description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a double-wing structure, painted in a muted pinkish-red color with white cross insignias on the upper wings and tail. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the rear, ending in a classic tail assembly with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, featuring a single seat and visible flight controls. The propeller is mounted at the nose, constructed from wood, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The wings are supported by red struts and cross-bracing wires, giving the aircraft a sturdy, classic appearance. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military or stunt aircraft, with a focus on lightweight construction and maneuverability.
description: A vintage pinkish-red biplane with double wings, open cockpit, wooden propeller, and classic tail design, featuring white cross insignias and red struts.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the twin horizontal stabilizers, single vertical stabilizer, and open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the double wings, red struts, open cockpit, and visible landing gear.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the wooden propeller, open cockpit, and symmetrical biplane wing structure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit, propeller, landing gear, and tail assembly with white cross insignias.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts and bracing wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers in a classic arrangement.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
